draft.title: ai workspace
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
ai workspace

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: The user prefers soft, ambient lighting and warm color temperatures in their AI workspace interface.
facade.label: Warm glow
facade.acceptImplies: Explore cozy, low-contrast interfaces with amber/gold accents, reduced blue light, inviting atmosphere
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer crisp, cool-toned, high-contrast interfaces with clarity over comfort

evidence.hypothesis: The user prefers soft, ambient lighting and warm color temperatures in their AI workspace interface.
evidence.implication: Explore cozy, low-contrast interfaces with amber/gold accents, reduced blue light, inviting atmosphere

facade.hypothesis: The user prefers warm, calming, intimate atmospheres over bright, clinical spaces
facade.axisTargeted: warm vs cool + calm vs energetic
facade.label: Soft shadows and dim light
facade.acceptImplies: Build with muted colors, gentle lighting, lower contrast — create a retreat-like feel
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer clarity and brightness — explore sharp contrast, bright surfaces, or energetic tones instead

facade.hypothesis: Users expect fluid, eased motion between workspace states (panel open/close, view switches) rather than instant snaps.
facade.label: Smooth slide transitions
facade.acceptImplies: Build transitions with easing curves; motion should feel like physical inertia, not teleportation.
facade.rejectImplies: Instant state changes are preferred; animations cost cognitive load and slow perceived responsiveness in a workspace tool.

facade.hypothesis: Users building AI workspaces prefer quantitative metrics displayed as live widgets over narrative summaries
facade.axisTargeted: Numbers vs narrative — do builders want to see KPIs at a glance or read context-rich descriptions?
facade.label: Number dashboard
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard-style info design: cards with counts, gauges, sparklines. Minimize prose. Favor scannability.
facade.rejectImplies: Narrative-forward design: prose explanations, story-driven onboarding, annotations over raw metrics. Context > counts.

facade.hypothesis: Users building an 'AI workspace' may prefer a personality that feels approachable and clever rather than stiff or corporate—balancing professionalism with moments of humor and warmth.
facade.axisTargeted: Friendly vs Professional + Playful vs Serious
facade.label: Casual and witty
facade.acceptImplies: The workspace voice should include conversational language, light humor, and personality that doesn't take itself too seriously. This lowers barrier to entry and builds rapport.
facade.rejectImplies: Users prefer a more reserved, authoritative, or polished tone. The workspace should feel trustworthy through expertise and formality rather than personality.

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer persistent navigation on the left over tab-switching for an AI workspace
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation persistence: always-visible sidebar vs collapsible/tab-based
facade.label: Sidebar always visible
facade.acceptImplies: Design should favor left-column navigation, fixed or sticky, with main content flowing right
facade.rejectImplies: Design should explore top tabs, collapsible sidebars, or full-width content with hidden navigation

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal, clean interfaces vs. rich visual detail and color
Whether user wants tools always visible vs. hidden/revealed on demand
Whether user responds to encouraging, conversational guidance vs. neutral, direct instruction
Whether user seeks calm, distraction-free focus vs. energetic, stimulating environment
Whether user prefers flat, simple information hierarchy vs. layered, detailed organization
Whether user wants instant feedback and snappy responses vs. smooth, deliberate transitions

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal, clean interfaces vs. rich visual detail and color
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants tools always visible vs. hidden/revealed on demand
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to encouraging, conversational guidance vs. neutral, direct instruction
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user seeks calm, distraction-free focus vs. energetic, stimulating environment
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers flat, simple information hierarchy vs. layered, detailed organization
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant feedback and snappy responses vs. smooth, deliberate transitions
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: The user may prefer clean, high-contrast designs with defined edges and sparse visual elements, contrasting with the warm, cozy aesthetic they hesitantly accepted.
facade.axisTargeted: Warmth vs. Coolness / Soft vs. Sharp (opposing to establish spectrum)
facade.label: Sharp, minimal interface
facade.acceptImplies: User values clarity and precision in their workspace; may appreciate dark mode with crisp typography, minimal ornamentation, and efficient layout
facade.rejectImplies: User strongly prefers the softer, warmer direction and dislikes stark or clinical interfaces; warm ambient aesthetic is core preference, not just hesitant acceptance

draft.title: AI Workspace Hub
draft.summary: A focused workspace interface for managing AI tasks, projects, and collaborative workflows. Clean, minimal design with warm neutrals and coral accents.
